title: WIN-013 c4 H3 (ISS-1541) is already-fixed by EXP-301 restructure — recommend close-no-patch
target:
data/wins.json:WIN-013 and data/sections.json:part6 (SEC-6.14) — no further patch required. The c4 hole #3 'trilemma horns stacked' structural concern raised by ISS-1541 (orphan-picked-up 2026-05-14, escalated to Opus deep-attack class) was already resolved by EXP-301 integration on 2026-05-10 (commits 3169656e and onward), which (a) retired the entire trilemma block from WIN-013 in favor of a clean empirical refutation, and (b) introduced SEC-6.14 with the 'two-horned forced choice' framing in the

The vise

subsection — implementing the c4 H3 recommendation option (a) ('reframe as a two-pronged dilemma') verbatim in spirit. Defense rating: c5 (post-EXP-301) found 7 holes all citation/precision, none touching trilemma framing; c6 found 1 hole range-cleanup only.
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-013.c4.json holes_found[2] (the originating c4 H3 'Trilemma horns labeled (a)/(b)/(c) but only (c) is independent'). Also referenced: WIN-013.c5.json (7 holes, none touching trilemma framing) and WIN-013.c6.json (1 hole, ISS-1944 follow-up range cleanup only). The c4 H3 recommendation was explicitly marked 'Defer to a future tightening pass — not blocking' by the c4 reviewer.
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:38Z; deep-attack class HONORED — close-no-patch warranted singleton review because verifying 'already-fixed-by-restructure' required tracing the c4 → EXP-301 → c5 → c6 chain plus literal grep of current sections.json for the implementing wording)
class_hint_honored: deep-attack (HONORED — tracker review_class=deep-attack; ISS-1541.class_hint=null per PROP-027 routing reason 'M1 sweep severity=moderate age=15d > 7d default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST'; deep-attack appropriate because the close-no-patch conclusion required adversarial cross-checking)
review_class_rationale:
Although the proposed action is close-no-patch (no text changes), the analytical work required: (i) reading three curmudgeon reviews (c4 H3, c5 H1-H7, c6 H1) to confirm no later reviewer re-flagged the trilemma framing; (ii) grepping data/wins.json:WIN-013 for trilemma/horn/dilemma/three-way/two-pronged terms (zero hits); (iii) grepping data/sections.json:SEC-6.14 for the implementing two-horned wording (found 'two-horned forced choice' under

The vise

, inside the p6-614-kappa-cluster anchor); (iv) reading EXP-301-kappa-cluster-section-and-WIN-013-014-reframe.json to confirm the restructure was the mechanism that retired the trilemma framing; (v) verifying via git log -S that 'two-horned forced choice' was introduced in commit 3169656e (Workspace sync 2026-05-10T15:06:49Z) — 15 days after ISS-1541 was opened (2026-04-25). Singleton review appropriate because close-no-patch on a moderate severity ISS with a 'defer not blocking' c4 disposition has nontrivial alternatives warranting adversarial scrutiny.
summary_for_decider:
ISS-1541 (WIN-013 c4 H3, moderate, orphan-picked-up 2026-05-14, escalated to Opus deep-attack) is already-fixed-by-restructure. C4 H3 hole: 'Trilemma horns labeled (a)/(b)/(c) but only (c) is independent; horns (a) and (b) are both consequences of the same EM interpretation; the label "three-way contradiction" / "each horn of this trilemma" overstates the independence of the horns.' Fix mechanism: EXP-301 (operator-commissioned via HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001) was integrated by decider on 2026-05-10T14:15:29Z (closing ISS-1870 explicitly, fixed_by=EXP-301-integrated). EXP-301 implemented the c4 H3 recommendation option (a) ('reframe as a two-pronged dilemma') via TWO mechanisms: 1. RETIRED the trilemma block from WIN-013 entirely. Verbatim grep of current data/wins.json:WIN-013 (pinned commit 3b6c7df57725) for 'trilemma|horn|dilemma|three-way|two-pronged|EM branch|EM interpretation' returns ZERO matches. WIN-013 detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence are now scoped narrowly to the empirical refutation: 'Under the dome's own published coupling κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the documented 1999 magnetic perturbation at Membach-co-located observatories predicts a contact-phase gravity pulse of order 3–6 µGal — well above the SG's 0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor. The Membach SG, continuously sampling across C1/C4, recorded zero.' Defense-neutralization (subsurface conductivity, Chebychev filter, mechanism shift) is now cross-referenced to §6.14's vise subsection. The c4 H3 concern is moot at the WIN level because no trilemma exists in WIN-013 to count horns on. 2. MOVED the structural argument to SEC-6.14 (data/sections.json:part6, anchor p6-614-kappa-cluster) where it lives in

The vise

with the explicit wording 'This produces a two-horned forced choice the dome cannot escape on its current published material.' The two horns are enumerated: (a) κ applies generally → 1999/2009 SG nulls refute the prediction outright; (b) κ has scope conditions exempting 1999/2009 → those same conditions destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation. This is exactly the c4 H3 recommendation option (a) — a clean two-pronged dilemma where each branch costs the model something concrete. The third 'horn' the c4 H3 reviewer worried about (EM-zeros-Δg vs EM-shouldn't-show-on-gravimeter) is now distributed: instrument-response → WIN-011 'Mohe quality' verdict Not Demonstrated; coupling-constant arithmetic → WIN-012 'κ definition' verdict Self-Contradicted; both feed SEC-6.14 as cross-references rather than competing horns within one WIN. C5 review (2026-05-10T18:51:15.653Z, post-EXP-301) flagged 7 holes — ALL citation/precision (Van Camp DOI 404, Tomás link mismatch, Domain #7 vs Domain 5 dashboard naming, Hvoždara ResearchGate link, tldr 5–10 nT vs detail 5–15 nT, κ precision 1.67 vs 1.677, WIN-011/012 cross-reference) — NONE re-flagging the trilemma/horn structural concern. C5 holes have separately been closed via ISS-1944, ISS-1946, ISS-1947. C6 review (2026-05-11T06:40Z, post-ISS-1944 patch) flagged 1 hole — internal range/prediction inconsistency from the ISS-1944 follow-up patch — no trilemma/horn concern. Live verification this run (clone @ commit 3b6c7df57725, 2026-05-15): - data/wins.json:WIN-013 all fields (claim/finding/detail_claim/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/detail_extra/tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict): contain no 'trilemma|horn|dilemma|three-way|two-pronged' (verified by full-field regex scan) - data/sections.json:part6 anchor p6-614-kappa-cluster: contains 'two-horned forced choice' at character index 108499 of part6.html (anchor opens at 98533, so wording lives inside SEC-6.14 boundaries — verified) - Two horns enumerated in SEC-6.14 prose; closing sentence: 'Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.' RECOMMENDED ACTION: close ISS-1541 as already-fixed-by-related (EXP-301). No patch required. Cross-reference EXP-301 and ISS-1870 (the verdict-upgrade ISS that EXP-301 integration explicitly closed) in closure note for audit trail. OUT-OF-SCOPE OBSERVATIONS (flagged for tracker visibility, do not block closure): (a) the 'two-horned' wording lives under

The vise

rather than in the SEC-6.14 ps-tldr — a section-nav skimmer sees the structural-summary tldr but not the explicit two-pronged framing. This is wordsmith polish, not a c4 H3 residual: the c4 H3 concern was rhetorical overstatement, not heading visibility; the SEC-6.14 tldr is structurally accurate. (b) 'The vise' subsection mixes 'horns' and 'vise' metaphors — intentional rhetoric, not a flaw. Neither blocks closure.

decision.rationale: Option C (verify the c4 H3 trilemma framing is retired and SEC-6.14 implements the c4 H3 recommendation, recommend close-no-patch with explicit EXP-301 cross-reference) is correct: (1) zero trilemma/horn/dilemma language remains in WIN-013 — the entire block the c4 H3 reviewer was attacking has been deleted; (2) SEC-6.14 now carries the structural argument with 'two-horned forced choice' wording — verbatim implementation of c4 H3 recommendation option (a); (3) two subsequent curmudgeon cycles (c5, c6) reviewed the post-EXP-301 WIN-013 and SEC-6.14 and found NO trilemma/horn structural issue; their holes are about citations and precision, not framing; (4) the c4 H3 itself was explicitly 'Defer to a future tightening pass — not blocking,' which the restructure has now answered with a more thorough fix than the c4 reviewer envisioned (full retirement plus relocation); (5) ISS-1541 is structurally a stale leftover of the c4-era WIN-013 architecture; the EXP-301 architecture supersedes it. Closing as already-fixed-by-related is the audit-trail-clean closure path.
decision.why_not_option_a: Option A (write a fresh patch that adds two-pronged dilemma framing to WIN-013 directly) rejected: (i) WIN-013 detail prose is now correctly scoped to the empirical refutation; adding back a structural-argument framing would re-introduce the kind of cross-WIN entanglement the EXP-301 architecture was designed to remove; (ii) c5 (post-EXP-301) flagged 7 specific holes and trilemma/horn structure was not among them — patching back what the architecture deliberately removed is a regression; (iii) the c4 H3 reviewer's recommendation is satisfied at the SEC-6.14 level, not the WIN-013 level; patching at WIN-013 would duplicate the argument across two surfaces.
decision.why_not_option_b:
Option B (re-scope ISS-1541 to track surviving SEC-6.14 wordsmith polish — e.g., promote 'two-horned forced choice' from

The vise

to the SEC-6.14 ps-tldr) rejected: (i) c4 H3 description specifically targets the WIN-013 trilemma block, not section-nav telegraphing; (ii) the SEC-6.14 ps-tldr already accurately summarizes the structural argument; re-scoping would be goalpost-moving — the c4 H3 concern is structurally satisfied, not just literally satisfied; (iii) wordsmith polish on the SEC-6.14 tldr should be a separate, freshly-scoped ISS if curmudgeon raises it (none has).
decision.why_not_option_d: Option D (Mode-2b OBE without EXP) rejected: (i) orphan-pickup escalation to Opus deep-attack signals tracker considered this ISS to need substantive analyst-level review, not mechanical OBE; (ii) Mode-2b OBE-triage is for attention-inbox items, not tracker-pending expansion items routed via orphan-pickup; (iii) close-no-patch recommendation here has non-trivial audit trail (EXP-301 cross-reference + c5/c6 verification + verbatim grep) an OBE one-liner would not capture; (iv) decider needs explicit issue_resolution proposal to close cleanly; (v) the linkage WIN-013-c4-H3 → EXP-301-restructure → ISS-1870-closure is an architectural-equivalence chain that an OBE pattern-matcher would not catch.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: Your reviewers wrote 'three-way contradiction' / 'each horn of this trilemma' and that overstates the independence — horns (a) and (b) are both consequences of the EM interpretation, not independent.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: The c4 H3 critique was correct against the c4-era WIN-013 text and we accept it. The fix landed: EXP-301 (operator-commissioned, integrated 2026-05-10) deleted the entire trilemma block from WIN-013 in favor of a clean empirical refutation, and moved the structural argument to SEC-6.14 where it is now framed as a 'two-horned forced choice' — verbatim two horns, not three. The argument that originally lived as horns (a) and (b) is now distributed: WIN-011 owns the spring-gravimeter EM-response problem (Not Demonstrated verdict), WIN-012 owns the κ-definition arithmetic problem (Self-Contradicted verdict), and SEC-6.14 owns the cross-eclipse vise structural argument. No reviewer can credibly accuse us of 'stacking consequences of one branch' in current text — the two SEC-6.14 horns are (i) κ-applies-generally → 1999/2009 SG nulls refute, and (ii) κ-has-scope-conditions → PRED-TIER-3 derivation collapses. Each branch costs the model a different concrete thing.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: ISS-1541 was opened 2026-04-25 against c4 H3; EXP-301 landed 2026-05-10 (15 days later); how can a fix that pre-dates the orphan-pickup chain not have closed ISS-1541 alongside ISS-1870?
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Because the tracker did not register the linkage at the time. ISS-1870 (the operator-commissioned verdict-upgrade ISS) was explicitly closed as fixed_by EXP-301 on 2026-05-10T14:15:29Z, but ISS-1541 (the older c4 H3 structural critique) was not cross-referenced in that closure batch — EXP-301's primary scope was 'verdict reframe + new section' per HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001, and the trilemma block retirement was a derived consequence of the verdict reframe rather than an explicit goal. The orphan-pickup chain (analyst-baby 2026-05-14, escalation to Opus 2026-05-14T11:38Z) surfaced the disposition gap 4 days after EXP-301 landed. This EXP closes the gap with fixed_by_exp=EXP-301 (or fixed_by_iss=ISS-1870) in the closed-issues note.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot:
The 'two-horned forced choice' wording sits inside

The vise

subsection of SEC-6.14 — a reader who only reads the SEC-6.14 ps-tldr will not see the explicit two-pronged dilemma framing.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal:
True but not a c4 H3 residual. The c4 H3 concern was structural — the rhetoric of 'three horns' overstating independence — and is fully answered by the current architecture: the SEC-6.14 vise is structurally two-pronged, the prose explicitly says so under

The vise

, the two horns are enumerated with concrete consequences each, and the closing sentence ('Both horns lose at least one WIN') ties the structural argument to the WIN-level verdicts. Telegraphing the two-pronged framing into the ps-tldr is a section-nav-visibility polish, not a structural critique; if it becomes a c-cycle concern, that's a future ISS, not an ISS-1541 residual. The c4 H3 reviewer themselves marked the recommendation 'Defer to a future tightening pass — not blocking,' which the EXP-301 restructure has now answered substantively rather than via in-place patch.

kernel_of_truth.acknowledged: The c4 H3 reviewer's structural critique was correct against the c4-era WIN-013 text — the trilemma horns (a) and (b) were both EM-interpretation consequences and calling them three independent horns did overstate the rhetoric. A careful defender reading the c4-era WIN-013 could have replied 'you stacked two consequences of one branch and called them three horns,' and that reply would have landed. The c4 reviewer saw the structural flaw before any defender did, which is exactly what an adversarial self-review process is supposed to surface. The orphan-pickup chain that re-surfaced ISS-1541 was also correct to flag a moderate-severity 15+-day-old assigned-analyst ISS for substantive review rather than mechanical OBE.
kernel_of_truth.why_does_not_save_dome: Not applicable in the typical sense (this EXP closes an internal tracker disposition gap, not a dome defense). However, the underlying scientific argument the c4 H3 was protecting — the structural critique of the κ coupling cluster — is now stronger after the EXP-301 restructure than it was before. The c4-era three-horn trilemma in WIN-013 was rhetorically vulnerable to the 'stacked consequences' rebuttal. The current two-horn vise in SEC-6.14 is not: branch (i) is a measurement-vs-prediction empirical refutation (κ predicts pulse, SG measured zero); branch (ii) is a derivation-collapse argument (any scope condition exempting 1999/2009 also exempts August 2026 and destroys PRED-TIER-3). These two branches cost the model concretely different things and cannot be collapsed into 'consequences of one position.' The dome's defender escape — 'electromagnetic, not gravitational' — does not bypass either branch: branch (i) holds because κ as published does couple ΔB to Δg, and branch (ii) holds because the August 2026 ΔB target itself comes from the same κ. The c4 H3 critique improved the review by forcing this clarification.

The vise

subsection (commit 3169656e Workspace sync 2026-05-10T15:06:49Z). c5/c6 reviewers (post-EXP-301) raised no trilemma/horn concerns. Verified verbatim by analyst-opus 2026-05-15 (clone @ commit 3b6c7df57725). No patch required.'.
